Comprehensive guide for creating software diagrams using Mermaid syntax. Use when users need to create, visualize, or document software through diagrams including class diagrams (domain modeling, object-oriented design), sequence diagrams (application flows, API interactions, code execution), flowcharts (processes, algorithms, user journeys), entity relationship diagrams (database schemas), C4 architecture diagrams (system context, containers, components), state diagrams, git graphs, pie charts, gantt charts, or any other diagram type. Triggers include requests to \"diagram\", \"visualize\", \"model\", \"map out\", \"show the flow\", or when explaining system architecture, database design, code structure, or user/application flows. # Mermaid Diagramming. Create professional software diagrams using Mermaid's text-based syntax. Mermaid renders diagrams from simple text definitions, making diagrams version-controllable, easy to update, and maintainable alongside code. 4. **Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERD)** - Database schemas. * **<references/class-diagrams.md>** - Domain modeling, relationships (association, composition, aggregation, inheritance), multiplicity, methods/properties. * **<references/erd-diagrams.md>** - Entities, relationships, cardinality, keys, attributes. * **Syntax errors** - Misspellings break diagrams; validate syntax in Mermaid Live. ## When to Create Diagrams."},{"title":"Mermaid Diagrams as Code in Notion - Luke Merrett","url":"https://lukemerrett.com/using-mermaid-flowchart-syntax-in-notion/","text":"Mermaid can create these types of diagram: Flowchart; Sequence Diagram; Gantt Chart; Class Diagram; Git Log Graph (experimental); Entity"},{"title":"Basic overview of creating flowcharts using Mermaid | CKEditor","url":"https://ckeditor.com/blog/basic-overview-of-creating-flowcharts-using-mermaid/","text":"The types of diagrams one can form with Mermaid include a flowchart, sequence diagram, class diagram, state diagram, entity-relationship diagram"},{"title":"Mermaid diagram: Create text-based flow charts and visuals | Tempo","url":"https://www.tempo.io/blog/mermaid-diagram","text":"Mermaid diagrams support multiple diagram types including flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, entity relationship diagrams, user"},{"title":"Learn Mermaid.js - SimpleMermaid","url":"https://simplemermaid.com/learn-mermaid.html","text":"Transform your ideas into beautiful diagrams with the simplest syntax you'll ever learn. A powerful, text-based diagramming tool that creates professional diagrams using simple, Markdown-like syntax. Write diagrams as code using intuitive syntax. ### 🔰 Your First Diagram. Every Mermaid diagram starts with declaring its type. flowchart TD A[Start] --> B[Process] B --> C[End]. This creates a top-down flowchart with three nodes connected by arrows. `A[Text]` creates a rectangle node with label \"Text\". `-->` creates an arrow between nodes. ## Diagram Types. flowchart LR Start --> Process Process --> Decision{Yes/No?} Decision -->|Yes| Success Decision -->|No| Retry. ### 🏗️ Class Diagram. ### 🔀 State Diagram. ## Connections & Arrows. flowchart TD A[Normal Node] B[Success]:::success C[Warning]:::warning D[Error]:::error classDef success fill:#5cb85c,stroke:#3e8e41,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff classDef warning fill:#f0ad4e,stroke:#ec971f,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff classDef error fill:#d9534f,stroke:#c9302c,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff A --> B A --> C A --> D. Instead of A, B, C, use meaningful names like User, Server, Database. Use subgraphs to organize related nodes visually. Start creating beautiful diagrams with what you've learned."},{"title":"Mermaid.js: The Code-First Approach to Technical Diagrams - Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@vaijrb/mermaid-js-the-code-first-approach-to-technical-diagrams-6a3c4247d842","text":"# Mermaid.js: The Code-First Approach to Technical Diagrams. Mermaid.js offers a different approach: diagrams as code. ## What is Mermaid.js. Mermaid.js is a JavaScript library that can generate diagrams and flowcharts from plain text easily and intuitively. With a syntax inspired by Markdown, Mermaid.js enables users to create a variety of diagrams, including flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, and many more. 2. **Supports multiple Diagram Types** Mermaid.js supports a wide range of diagram types. 4. **Interactive Elements:** Mermaid.js supports interactive elements such as tooltips and clickable links, enhancing the functionality and user experience of your diagrams. You can find more examples on the Mermaid.js documentation. 3. **Technical Documentation:** Technical writers can leverage Mermaid.js to create concise diagrams that complement their written content. For technical program managers and engineering leaders, Mermaid.js represents more than just a diagramming tool — it’s a strategic asset for documentation-as-code practices. **Start small**: add one Mermaid diagram to your next technical document or README."},{"title":"Mermaid.js: A Complete Guide - Swimm","url":"https://swimm.io/learn/mermaid-js/mermaid-js-a-complete-guide","text":"[Skip to content](https://swimm.io/learn/mermaid-js/mermaid-js-a-complete-guide#primary). Mermaid.js is a JavaScript library that provides an easy-to-use syntax for creating various types of diagrams and flowcharts. Mermaid.js is a versatile and feature-rich JavaScript library for creating diagrams and flowcharts using simple markdown-like text syntax. 3.   Customizable styling: Mermaid.js allows users to customize the styling of diagrams and flowcharts using CSS, including the colors, fonts, and sizes of nodes, edges, and other elements. 6.   Export to different formats: Mermaid.js allows users to export diagrams and flowcharts in various image formats, such as SVG, PNG, and PDF. Swimm is another tool where you can embed Mermaid diagrams, and it integrates with your codebase to keep documentation and visualizations up-to-date and accessible. Sequence diagrams, Gantt charts, and other diagram types have their specific syntax elements (described in[Mermaid’s documentation](https://mermaid.js.org/intro/)), but the principles remain the same: simple, human-readable text that generates visually appealing diagrams."},{"title":"Entity Relationship Diagram - Mermaid Flow","url":"https://www.mermaidflow.app/docs/syntax/entity-relationship-diagram","text":"# Entity Relationship Diagram. Define data models and relationships between entities in a database system. Entity Relationship Diagrams (ER Diagrams) are visual representations of entity-relationship models that describe interrelated things of interest in a specific domain of knowledge. A basic ER model consists of entity types that classify the things of interest and specifies relationships that can exist between entities (instances of those entity types). ### Entities and Relationships. * `relationship` describes how both entities relate to each other (see below). * `relationship-label` describes the relationship from the first entity's perspective. Only the `first-entity` part of a statement is mandatory, allowing you to show an entity with no relationships. 3. The cardinality of the second entity relative to the first. ### Entity Name Aliases. Aliases for entity names can be defined using square brackets:. Entity Relationship Diagrams can be extensively customized through configuration options to adjust the visual appearance and behavior of the diagram. * Entity names with spaces must be enclosed in double quotes."},{"title":"Mermaid Live Editor: Online FlowChart & Diagrams Editor","url":"https://mermaid.live/","text":"fa:fa-car Car]. All other diagram types will be rendered as an image in Excalidraw. - Unsupported shapes fallback to Rectangle."},{"title":"Specification [#section] - JSON Schema","url":"https://json-schema.org/specification","text":"| JSON Schema Validation | defines the validation keywords of JSON Schema |. ## Meta-schemas. The meta-schemas are schemas against which other schemas can be validated. It is self-descriptive: the JSON Schema meta-schema validates itself. The latest meta-schema is **2020-12**. Please note, additional vocabulary specific schema files are needed to fully construct and use the Core/Validation Dialect meta-schema. | Core/Validation Dialect meta-schema | Used for schemas written for pure validation. | Hyper-Schema Dialect meta-schema | Used for schemas written for validation (2020-12) and hyper-linking (2019-09). ## Single-vocabulary meta-schemas. These are relevant primarily to people who want to write their own meta-schemas that build on specific parts of the existing specification. * Format Annotation Vocabulary meta-schema. * Format Assertion Vocabulary meta-schema. * Meta-Data Vocabulary meta-schema. * JSON Schema recommended output schema. * JSON Schema verbose output example. * JSON Schema Core and Validation. + There was no JSON Hyper-Schema draft for 2020-12 releases.
